title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Delight
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Lamb Ouzi! This slow-roasted Middle Eastern lamb recipe is perfect for special occasions. Get the step-by-step guide here.
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Recipe
h2Headings: What is Lamb Ouzi? A Traditional Middle Eastern Dish
Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Lamb Ouzi
Tips for Perfectly Slow Roasted Lamb
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi dish, garnished with nuts and herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of this traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to perfection.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a majestic Middle Eastern dish, typically featuring slow-roasted lamb served over aromatic rice, often garnished with nuts and fresh herbs. This recipe guides you through assembling a flavorful Ouzi, perfect for celebrations or a grand family dinner. Experience the rich, savory tastes that define traditional Middle Eastern cuisine.

ingredients.item: Lamb shoulder, boneless, cooked and shredded

instructions.title: Prepare Rice and Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse basmati rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear; set aside to drain completely. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it softens and becomes translucent, which usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until it becomes fragrant.

instructions.title: Infuse Spices
instructions.text: Add the Ouzi spice blend, ground cinnamon, and ground cardamom to the pot. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, allowing the spices to toast lightly and become intensely aromatic. This step is crucial for developing the deep Middle Eastern flavors.

instructions.title: Cook the Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: Add the drained basmati rice to the pot, stirring gently to ensure each grain is evenly coated with the oil and spices. Pour in the chicken or lamb broth and season with salt and black pepper to your preference. Bring the mixture to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables and Rest
instructions.text: Once the rice is cooked, gently fold in the frozen peas and diced carrots. Cover the pot again and let the rice rest off the heat for an additional 10 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld beautifully and the added vegetables to warm through without overcooking. After resting, fluff the rice gently with a fork.

instructions.title: Prepare and Warm Lamb
instructions.text: While the rice is resting, ensure your pre-cooked slow-roasted lamb pieces are warmed through. You can gently reheat them in a separate pan over low heat or in a microwave. If using fresh lamb pieces, quickly sear them, then simmer in a little broth until tender, then shred or dice as desired.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: On a large, impressive serving platter, mound the aromatic Ouzi rice as the base. Artfully arrange the warm lamb pieces over the rice. Finally, garnish generously with the toasted slivered almonds, toasted pine nuts, and fresh chopped parsley for both visual appeal and added texture. Serve immediately as a magnificent centerpiece for your meal.

tips: For a richer flavor profile, always toast your slivered almonds and pine nuts in a dry pan until they are golden brown before using them as a garnish. This intensifies their nutty aroma and adds a delightful crunch.

The Ouzi spice blend is truly the heart of this dish. If you dont have a pre-made blend, you can create a similar aromatic profile by mixing equal parts allspice, cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg, and a pinch of black pepper.

To significantly reduce your cooking time on the day of serving, prepare the slow-roasted lamb shoulder a day in advance. Cooked lamb reheats beautifully and makes the final assembly of the Ouzi a quick and stress-free process.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East, where vibrant spices, succulent meats, and aromatic rice converge to create dishes of unparalleled flavour and festivity. Among these gastronomic treasures, one stands out for its grandeur, its tender perfection, and its ability to gather loved ones around a shared table: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted. This isn't just a meal; it's an experience, a celebration of time-honoured traditions and the art of slow cooking. If you're searching for Middle Eastern dinner recipes that truly impress, look no further than this magnificent dish.

## What is Lamb Ouzi? A Traditional Middle Eastern Dish

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzi" or "Ouziee," is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish renowned for its spectacular presentation and incredible depth of flavour. At its heart, Lamb Ouzi lies a whole leg or shoulder of lamb, slow-roasted until it’s fall-off-the-bone tender and infused with a symphony of spices. This succulent lamb is traditionally served atop a generous bed of fragrant basmati rice, often studded with peas, carrots, toasted nuts like almonds and pistachios, and sometimes dried fruits like raisins.

Originating from the Levant region, Ouzi is a dish reserved for special occasions, grand feasts, weddings, and religious holidays. It embodies hospitality and generosity, often presented on a large communal platter as the centrepiece of a celebratory spread. The magic of Ouzi comes from the contrast between the melt-in-your-mouth lamb and the richly spiced, slightly crunchy rice, all coming together in a harmonious blend that tantalizes the senses. While the core elements remain consistent, regional variations exist, each adding its unique touch to this beloved classic.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

To prepare a truly authentic whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, you’ll need a selection of high-quality ingredients, patience, and a passion for flavour.

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb leg or shoulder (bone-in, 6-8 lbs / 2.7-3.6 kg)
*   Marinade:
    *   1 cup plain full-fat yogurt
    *   4-6 cloves garlic, minced
    *   Juice of 1 large lemon
    *   2 tbsp olive oil
    *   1 tbsp ground cumin
    *   1 tbsp ground coriander
    *   1 tbsp sweet paprika
    *   1 tsp ground turmeric
    *   1 tsp ground cinnamon
    *   1/2 tsp ground cardamom
    *   1/2 tsp ground nutmeg
    *   Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

For the Aromatic Rice:
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   6 cups rich lamb or chicken stock
*   1/4 cup olive oil or ghee
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   3 cloves garlic, minced
*   1 tbsp Ouzi spice blend (or a mix of cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg)
*   1/2 cup frozen peas
*   1/2 cup diced carrots
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, toasted
*   1/4 cup pine nuts, toasted
*   Optional: 1/4 cup golden raisins or dried barberries
*   Salt and pepper to taste

For Garnish:
*   Fresh parsley or mint, chopped
*   Extra toasted nuts

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Lamb Ouzi involves careful preparation and, most importantly, the art of slow roasting.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:**
    *   Pat the lamb leg dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the fatty side of the lamb deeply in a crosshatch pattern, being careful not to cut into the meat itself.
    *   In a bowl, whisk together all marinade ingredients. Rub the marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scored crevices.
    *   Place the marinated lamb in a large roasting pan, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 12 hours, preferably 24 hours.

2.  **Slow Roast the Lamb:**
    *   Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C).
    *   Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for about an hour.
    *   Add 1-2 cups of water or stock to the bottom of the roasting pan to create moisture. Cover the pan very tightly with foil (you might need a double layer).
    *   Roast for 4-6 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and an internal temperature of 200°F (93°C) is reached. The cooking time will vary depending on the size of your lamb.
    *   For the last 30-45 minutes, remove the foil and increase the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) to allow the lamb to brown and crisp up beautifully.
    *   Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a carving board, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for at least 30 minutes before carving. This resting period is crucial for juicy, tender meat.

3.  **Prepare the Aromatic Rice:**
    *   While the lamb is resting, heat olive oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the chopped onion until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
    *   Stir in the Ouzi spice blend and cook for 1 minute until aromatic. Add the rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat every grain with the spices and oil.
    *   Pour in the lamb or chicken stock, season with salt and pepper, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
    *   Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 10 minutes.
    *   Gently fluff the rice with a fork. Stir in the frozen peas, diced carrots, toasted slivered almonds, pine nuts, and optional raisins/barberries.

## Tips for Perfectly Slow Roasted Lamb

Mastering the slow roasting technique for Lamb Ouzi is key to its success. Here are a few pointers:

*   **Quality Lamb:** Start with a good quality, bone-in lamb leg or shoulder. The bone adds flavour and helps the meat cook more evenly.
*   **Long Marination:** Don't skimp on the marinating time. The longer the lamb marinates, the more flavourful and tender it will become.
*   **Low and Slow:** The secret to melt-in-your-mouth lamb is a low oven temperature over an extended period. This breaks down connective tissues slowly, resulting in incredible tenderness.
*   **Keep it Moist:** Covering the roasting pan tightly with foil traps steam, preventing the lamb from drying out during its long cook time. A little liquid at the bottom of the pan also helps.
*   **Resting is Essential:** Never skip the resting period. It allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring every slice is succulent.
*   **Meat Thermometer:** For absolute precision, use a meat thermometer. A perfectly cooked lamb leg for Ouzi should reach an internal temperature of around 200°F (93°C) for fall-apart tenderness.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

The presentation of Lamb Ouzi is as important as its taste. Traditionally, it’s a communal dish, designed to be shared and admired.

*   **Grand Platter:** Arrange the fluffy, aromatic rice on a large serving platter, creating a beautiful bed. Carefully place the whole slow roasted lamb leg on top of the rice.
*   **Garnish Generously:** Sprinkle generously with fresh chopped parsley or mint and extra toasted nuts (almonds, pine nuts, pistachios) for added crunch and visual appeal.
*   **Accompaniments:** Serve alongside a simple fresh green salad dressed with lemon and olive oil, a bowl of cooling labneh or plain yogurt, and perhaps some pickled vegetables. A refreshing cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like a Middle Eastern raita) also complements the rich flavours beautifully.
*   **Communal Experience:** Encourage guests to help themselves directly from the platter, fostering a warm, communal dining experience.

## Variations and Substitutions for Ouzi

While this guide focuses on the classic Lamb Ouzi, there are many delicious variations and substitutions you can explore:

*   **Meat Options:** If a whole lamb leg is too much, you can adapt this recipe for smaller cuts like lamb shoulder, lamb shanks, or even a bone-in leg of goat. For a quicker version, some recipes use chicken, though the flavour profile will be different.
*   **Rice Fillings:** Experiment with different vegetables in the rice, such as bell peppers, chickpeas, or even diced potatoes. The spice blend for the rice can also be adjusted to your preference – some prefer more cinnamon, others more cardamom.
*   **Stuffing the Lamb:** In some traditional preparations, the lamb is deboned and then stuffed with the spiced rice mixture before being rolled and slow-roasted. This creates an impressive cross-section when sliced.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi:** While not traditional, a delicious vegetarian version can be made by focusing on the aromatic rice with a richer vegetable stock, and perhaps adding roasted vegetables like eggplant or chickpeas for substance.
*   **Garnishes:** Beyond nuts and herbs, you can add pomegranate seeds for a burst of tartness and colour, or a drizzle of pomegranate molasses for a sweet-sour finish.

Preparing a whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi is a labour of love, but the rewards are immense. The intoxicating aroma filling your home, the tender, flavourful lamb, and the vibrant, spiced rice create an unforgettable meal. This Middle Eastern dinner recipe is more than just food; it's an invitation to celebrate, to share, and to savour the rich tapestry of Middle Eastern culinary heritage. Embrace the slow process, and you’ll be rewarded with a feast that will be talked about for years to come.
